name: Amari
bio:
Amari is a bright, energetic 10-year-old who loves sports like flag football, basketball, WWE, and any chance to compete. One morning, a wrong-way driver hit Amari and his father head-on while they were on their way to school. The accident left Amari paralyzed and without his right leg. Since then, Amari and his family have been adjusting to a new way of life, and Sunshine on a Ranney Day has been honored to support them. Thanks to the generous help of former Atlanta Hawks, Trae Young, the Trae Young Family Foundation, and the talented Crosby Design Group, SOARD transformed Amari's home with a fully wheelchair-accessible bathroom and a custom dream bedroom. Now, Amari can move independently between floors using the new stair lift, take showers safely in a bathroom designed for his needs, and keep his favorite things within reach in his new bedroom. These changes mean he no longer needs to rely on others for basic tasks, allowing him to spend more time with his family and focus on the activities he loves. This has given him back a sense of independence, comfort, and possibility. The project reached a national audience when a special story about Amari's makeover was shown nationally on the NBC Nightly News, just days before Christmas.

In April 2025, former Atlanta Hawks star Trae Young visited Amari in the hospital following his accident. Inspired by Amari's spirit and his family's strength, Trae wanted to do more than just visit, he wanted to make a real difference. He reached out to SOARD, and together with Crosby Design Group, we created spaces to make Amari's new life only manageable but meaningful.

In October 2025, Trae, Amari, and our team gathered at Walk Your Plans Atlanta, where Amari saw his new bathroom and bedroom mapped out full scale for the first time. In November the team from Kids R Kids Corporate worked with Amari and SOARD project manager Christian to clear out the bathroom and bedroom and start construction.

Jennifer Crosby of Crosby Design Group and junior designer Ella Guinn redesigned the bathroom layout to create more space to move, turn easily, and use the shower safely. Because Amari shares the bathroom with his sister, they added thoughtful storage solutions to make the space comfortable and useful for both of them.Once again, our partners made a big difference. Mohawk Flooring provided the new flooring for Amari's room. MSI supplied the bathroom tile. CR Home and Cambria delivered the vanity countertops. Real Floors Commercial went above and beyond by donating the Schluter system and all the labor for both the flooring and tile installation. These relationships make our work possible, and we are truly grateful for partners who support us with generosity and heart.

Crosby Design Group ensured Amari's personality shone throughout his bedroom. They included a unique bed shaped like a shoebox and a custom-built-in closet and desk made just for him. Mike Kamber and the Therapy Gyms team built the custom bed, while Echols Glass & Mirror created the closet and desk that complete the space.

We are truly grateful to Trae Young, the Trae Young Family Foundation, Crosby Design Group, and every partner who supported Amari and his family. Thanks to you, Amari now has a home where he can heal, grow, and regain his independence. His family can also move forward with more ease, comfort, and hope.
